name: ToggleGroup
description: A group of two-state toggle buttons. Supports single-select (radiogroup semantics) and multi-select (group semantics) modes with roving tabindex keyboard navigation. Built on Base UI ToggleGroup with StyleX styling.

Root.description: Container that manages pressed state across child items. Renders role="radiogroup" in single mode and role="group" in multiple mode.

type.description: Selection mode. 'single' allows one or zero pressed items; 'multiple' allows any combination.

value.description: Controlled pressed value. String (or null) for single mode; array for multiple.

onValueChange.description: Callback fired when the pressed value changes. Signature depends on type.

orientation.description: Layout direction. Determines whether ArrowLeft/Right or ArrowUp/Down move focus.

disabled.description: Whether all items in the group should ignore user interaction.

loopFocus.description: Whether arrow-key focus loops at the ends of the group.

dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the group is disabled.

Item.description: An individual two-state toggle button inside the group. Currently implemented inline; will be refactored to import the standalone Toggle primitive once the parallel Toggle PR lands.

value.description: Unique value identifying this item within the group.

disabled.description: Whether this individual item should ignore user interaction.

dataAttributes.data-pressed: Present when the item is pressed.
dataAttributes.data-unpressed: Present when the item is not pressed.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the item is disabled.

intents.reasoning: Single-mode ToggleGroup is ideal for mutually exclusive views (e.g. list vs grid), alignment pickers, or tabs-like switchers where one item is pressed at a time. Exposes radiogroup semantics for AT.
Multi-mode ToggleGroup fits formatting toolbars or tag/filter pickers where any combination of items can be pressed simultaneously.

avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Toggle directly. ToggleGroup adds shared-state overhead for a single item.

avoidWhen.scenario: Navigation between page-level views with associated content panels
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Tabs. Tabs ties triggers to panels and exposes the correct ARIA relationship; ToggleGroup is for stateful buttons without panels.

avoidWhen.scenario: Form selection where the value is submitted as a form field
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Radio (single) or CheckboxGroup (multiple). ToggleGroup buttons don't participate in native form submission.

examples.name: vertical
examples.code:
<ToggleGroup.Root type="single" orientation="vertical" defaultValue="a">
  <ToggleGroup.Item value="a">A</ToggleGroup.Item>
  <ToggleGroup.Item value="b">B</ToggleGroup.Item>
  <ToggleGroup.Item value="c">C</ToggleGroup.Item>
</ToggleGroup.Root>

examples.name: controlled
examples.code:
const [view, setView] = useState<string | null>('list');

<ToggleGroup.Root type="single" value={view} onValueChange={setView}>
  <ToggleGroup.Item value="list">List</ToggleGroup.Item>
  <ToggleGroup.Item value="grid">Grid</ToggleGroup.Item>
</ToggleGroup.Root>
